title: Outcome Contract, Non-Goals, and Measures of Success · Decision Evidence Lens: Guardrail Metric
summary: The proposed product promises engagement and delight but names no user decision, baseline, guardrail, accessibility outcome, or unacceptable harm. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp1 guardrail metric increment in measurable outcome and non-goal contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pair success measures with accessibility, equity, privacy, security, reliability, and burden guardrails.
Define a baseline, observable leading and lagging measures, collection method, comparison window, and uncertainty.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Guardrail Metric · Theory Guardrail Metric
steps.instruction:
theory case — A team targets engagement growth without a baseline, causal path, burden guardrail, or threshold for stopping a harmful direction. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Pair success measures with accessibility, equity, privacy, security, reliability, and burden guardrails.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Guardrail Metric contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Guardrail Metric · Theory Guardrail Metric.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for pair success measures with accessibility, equity, privacy, security, reliability, and burden guardrails. For Predict Guardrail Metric · Theory Guardrail Metric, keep Guardrail Metric connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A team targets engagement growth without a baseline, causal path, burden guardrail, or threshold for stopping a harmful direction. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Pair success measures with accessibility, equity, privacy, security, reliability, and burden guardrails.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Guardrail Metric condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: ACM IEEE AAAI Computer Science Curricula 2023 — Controls competency-based progression, project work, professional practice, ethics, accessibility, security, testing, and the integration of knowledge with observable performance.

hints: For Predict Guardrail Metric · Theory Guardrail Metric, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Guardrail Metric precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Predict Guardrail Metric · Theory Guardrail Metric, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A team targets engagement growth without a baseline, causal path, burden guardrail, or threshold for stopping a harmful direction. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Pair success measures with accessibility, equity, privacy, security, reliability, and burden guardrails.

For Predict Guardrail Metric · Theory Guardrail Metric, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Optimizing the primary metric cannot make the product worse overall.

options.text: Pair success measures with accessibility, equity, privacy, security, reliability, and burden guardrails. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
Optimizing the primary metric cannot make the product worse overall. That claim does not survive the predict guardrail metric evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Bound Guardrail Metric · Theory Guardrail Metric
steps.instruction:
theory case — A team targets engagement growth without a baseline, causal path, burden guardrail, or threshold for stopping a harmful direction. After tracing the worked code, choose the Guardrail Metric application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.

Evidence target: Bound Guardrail Metric · Theory Guardrail Metric.

content.text: Pair success measures with accessibility, equity, privacy, security, reliability, and burden guardrails. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A team targets engagement growth without a baseline, causal path, burden guardrail, or threshold for stopping a harmful direction. The worked example variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Pair success measures with accessibility, equity, privacy, security, reliability, and burden guardrails. A defensible solution applies Guardrail Metric, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to measurable outcome and non-goal contract.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-2
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Optimizing the primary metric cannot make the product worse overall. This fails the Guardrail Metric contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: ACM IEEE AAAI Computer Science Curricula 2023 (CS2023 final report) — Controls competency-based progression, project work, professional practice, ethics, accessibility, security, testing, and the integration of knowledge with observable performance.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pair success measures with accessibility, equity, privacy, security, reliability, and burden guardrails.

stimulus.title: Guardrail Metric failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Optimizing the primary metric cannot make the product worse overall.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pair success measures with accessibility, equity, privacy, security, reliability, and burden guardrails.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Guardrail Metric, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the guardrail metric defect evidence boundary.

steps.title: Defend the Guardrail Metric model · Theory Guardrail Metric
steps.instruction:

The theory variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Pair success measures with accessibility, equity, privacy, security, reliability, and burden guardrails.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Pair success measures with accessibility, equity, privacy, security, reliability, and burden guardrails.
